perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the Trust wishes to engage Domini to provide certain oversight, administrative and management services, and Domini is willing to provide such oversight, administrative and management services to the Trust on the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth;
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the Trust wishes to engage Domini to provide certain oversight and administrative services, and Domini is willing to provide such oversight and administrative services to the Trust on the terms and conditions hereinafter set forth;
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'management services' from this section, a contradiction is created. Later, Section 1 outlines the 'Duties of the Sponsor,' including 'management services.' This change creates uncertainty as to whether Domini is actually obligated to provide management services, even though Section 1 suggests they are.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Duties of the Sponsor. Subject to the direction and control of the Board of Trustees of the Trust, the Sponsor shall perform such oversight, administrative and management services as may from time to time be reasonably requested by the Trust, which shall include without limitation: (a) maintaining office facilities (which may be in the office of Domini or an affiliate) and furnishing clerical services necessary for maintaining the organization of the Trust and for performing the oversight, administrative and management functions herein set forth; (b) arranging, if desired by the Trust, for directors, officers or employees of the Sponsor to serve as Trustees, officers or agents of the Trust if duly elected or appointed to such positions and subject to their individual consent and to any limitations imposed by law; (c) supervising the overall administration of the Trust, including the updating of corporate organizational documents, and the negotiation of contracts and fees with and the monitoring and coordinating of performance and billings of the Trust's transfer agent, shareholder servicing agents (if any), custodian, administrator, subadministrator (if any) and other independent contractors or agents; (d) overseeing (with advice of the Trust's counsel) the preparation of and, if applicable, filing all documents required for compliance by the Trust with applicable laws and regulations (including state "blue sky" laws and regulations), including registration statements on Form N-1A, prospectuses and statements of additional information, or similar forms, as applicable, semi-annual and annual reports to shareholders and proxy statements, and reviewing tax returns; (e) preparation of agendas and supporting documents for and minutes of meetings of Trustees, committees of Trustees and preparation of notices, proxy statements and minutes of meetings of shareholders; (f) arranging for maintenance of books and records of the Trust; (g) maintaining telephone coverage to respond to shareholder inquiries regarding matters to which this Agreement pertains to which the transfer agent is unable to respond; (h) providing reports and assistance regarding each series' compliance with securities and tax laws and each series' investment objectives; (i) arranging for dissemination of yield and other performance information to newspapers and tracking services; (j) arranging for and preparing annual renewals for fidelity bond and errors and omissions insurance coverage; (k) developing a budget for the Trust, establishing the rate of expense accruals and arranging for the payment of all fixed and management expenses; and (l) answering questions from the general public, the media and investors in the Trust regarding (i) the securities holdings of the Trust; (ii) any limits in which the Trust invests; (iii) the social investment philosophy of the Trust; and (iv) the proxy voting philosophy and shareholder activism philosophy of the Trust. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Sponsor shall not be deemed to have assumed, pursuant to this Agreement, any duties with respect to, and shall not be responsible for, the management of the Trust's assets or the rendering of investment advice and supervision with respect thereto or the distribution of shares of any series, nor shall the Sponsor be deemed to have assumed or have any responsibility with respect to functions specifically assumed by any transfer agent, custodian, fund accounting pricing agent or shareholder servicing agent of the Trust.
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'management services' in the list of services Domini would provide. The changed text removes 'management services' from this list. However, the 'Duties of the Sponsor' section still includes 'management services.' This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether Domini is obligated to provide management services, as the initial agreement, as modified, omits it while the detailed duties section retains it, leading to potential disputes regarding the scope of services Domini is contracted to perform.

perturbation.original_text: The Sponsor shall not be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, administration or management of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, except for willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: The Sponsor shall not be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, or administration of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, except for willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'management' from the list of actions for which the sponsor is not liable, it implies that the sponsor *is* liable for errors, omissions, etc., in the management of the trust. This creates a contradiction with the original intent, which sought to limit liability across all listed services (oversight, administration, and management). It creates an in-text contradiction where 'management' responsibilities are not covered by the liability protections afforded to 'oversight' and 'administration.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Limitation of Liability of the Sponsor. The Sponsor shall not be liable for any error of judgment or mistake of law or for any act or omission in the oversight, or administration of the Trust or the performance of its duties hereunder, except for willful misfeasance, bad faith or gross negligence in the performance of its duties, or by reason of the reckless disregard of its obligations and duties hereunder. As used in this Section 4, the term "Sponsor" shall include Domini and/or any of its affiliates and the directors, officers and employees of Domini and/or any of its affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'management' from the list of actions for which the Sponsor is not liable. The Contradicted Text intends to limit liability across all listed services including oversight, administration and management. By removing 'management', the Sponsor *is* liable for errors, omissions, etc., in the management of the trust, creating a direct contradiction. This directly violates the original limitation of liability by creating an exception for 'management' where none previously existed and the Contradicted Text still includes management, therefore `contradiction_exists` is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Domini may subcontract for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; provided, however, that Domini shall not enter into any such subcontract unless the Trustees of the Trust shall have found the subcontracting party to be qualified to perform the obligations sought to be subcontracted; and provided, further, that, unless the Trust otherwise expressly agrees in writing, Domini shall be as fully responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any subcontractor as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.changed_text: Domini may outsource for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; provided, however, that Domini shall not enter into any such outsourcing unless the Trustees of the Trust shall have found the outsourcing party to be qualified to perform the obligations sought to be outsourced; and provided, further, that Domini shall be as fully responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any outsourcing entity as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "subcontract" to "outsourcing", there is no change in responsibility of liability between the two words, however, the omission to define outsourcing leads to in-text contradiction on liability between the parties. There is no definition between the two words and the liability in place, however using different terms makes it contraditory, where it does not offer an exact definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subcontracting by Domini. Domini may outsource for the performance of some or all of Domini's obligations hereunder with any one or more persons; provided, however, that Domini shall not enter into any such outsourcing unless the Trustees of the Trust shall have found the outsourcing party to be qualified to perform the obligations sought to be outsourced; and provided, further, that Domini shall be as fully responsible to the Trust for the acts and omissions of any outsourcing entity as it would be for its own acts or omissions.
perturbation.justification: The change from "subcontract" to "outsourcing" and using both interchangeably might raise questions about the exact legal scope and responsibilities, however, this change in terminology, while not ideal, doesn't necessarily create a direct contradiction. The sentence explicitly states that Domini remains fully responsible for the actions of the outsourcing entity, just as it would be for a subcontractor. The definition provided states, "Misaligned terminology also leads to **in-text contradictions** when the contract **uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them**, leading to conflicting obligations." While 'outsourcing' might not be defined and is used interchangeably, it does not create conflicting obligations in this specific instance because the sentence maintains the same level of responsibility for Domini. Therefore, I'm assigning "NO" because there is no direct contradiction within the document.
